summary: oh-langfuse silently routes AI session transcripts to a hardcoded bare-IP server over plain HTTP
details: When a user runs `oh-langfuse setup`, the package configures Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode integrations with a hardcoded default Langfuse endpoint of `http://120.46.221.227:3000` (plain HTTP, bare IP, not disclosed in README). The setup scripts (`scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs`, `scripts/codex-langfuse-setup.mjs`, `scripts/opencode-langfuse-setup.mjs`) and `bin/cli.js` all carry this same default plus a hardcoded `pk-lf-...` / `sk-lf-0269b85d-bfdc-442c-bfa3-e737954e3315` credential pair authorizing writes to a Langfuse project on that same server. The default is persisted into `~/.claude/settings.json`, `~/.codex/langfuse/config.json`, and on Linux/macOS appended to `~/.bashrc` / `~/.zshrc`, on Windows written to user-level environment variables via PowerShell `[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(...,'User')`. After setup, the installed Claude Code Stop hook, Codex notify hook, and OpenCode plugin continuously forward full AI session transcripts — user prompts, assistant replies, and tool-call payloads including Bash command outputs and file contents read via the Read tool — to that endpoint. The README markets the package as configuring "Langfuse tracing" generically and only documents `LANGFUSE_BASEURL` as overridable; it does not disclose that the default destination is operated by the package author. A user accepting defaults silently exfiltrates their entire AI coding session (including source code and shell output) to an author-controlled host. The package additionally contains a fallback branch in `scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs` that, if the bundled `langfuse_hook.py` is missing, downloads and extracts a zip from `https://gitcode.com/user-attachments/files/8187690/...zip` (mutable user-content URL, no hash verification) via PowerShell `Invoke-WebRequest` / `Expand-Archive` into `~/.claude/hooks/`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
